skill.name: specdd-test
skill.description: Derive, add, update, and assess focused tests from active SpecDD project specifications.
skill.summary: Connects SpecDD contracts to focused verification work.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby or shell backtick execution are false positives from Markdown inline code and list text. The weak cryptography alert on SKILL.md line 3 is also a textual false positive, while line 50 permits running project test commands as a legitimate testing action.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Markdown inline code on SKILL.md lines 13, 15, 21, 22, 23, 37, and 48 as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines describe files and SpecDD keywords, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged tokens appear in Markdown prose and inline code formatting. No Ruby, shell script, or executable command syntax is present at these locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Is a Text Match
low_findings.description: The high static alert on SKILL.md line 3 appears to match text inside the word description or related prose. No cryptographic API, algorithm selection, or security implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is front matter describing when to use the skill. It does not contain cryptographic code or instructions to use a weak algorithm.

low_findings.title: Project Test Command May Be Run
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run the relevant project test command when available. This is legitimate for test verification, but it can execute project-defined commands in the local environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction clearly allows running tests, but it does not provide a fixed command or unsafe argument construction. Risk depends on the target project's test configuration.

content.user_title: Derive SpecDD Tests from Active Specs
content.value_statement: SpecDD projects need tests that trace directly to active contract text. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused verification work.
actual_capabilities: Reads the active SpecDD bootstrap chain before test planning.
Resolves governing specs for a target path, behavior, or task.
Derives test cases from SpecDD entries such as Must and Scenario.
Identifies the nearest spec that grants write authority before edits.
Reports which spec entries each test covers.
Notes specified behavior that still lacks verification.
limitations: Does not adopt SpecDD for projects that lack SpecDD setup.
May need user approval when spec authority is unclear.
Relies on project test commands when available.

use_cases.title: Add Contract-Based Tests
use_cases.description: Create focused tests that prove behavior required by active SpecDD specs.

use_cases.title: Assess Verification Coverage
use_cases.description: Compare existing tests against SpecDD requirements and identify uncovered behavior.

use_cases.title: Review Test Authority
use_cases.description: Confirm whether a spec grants permission to edit a test file before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Find Missing Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-test to inspect the active specs for this target and list behavior that lacks tests.

prompt_templates.title: Add Focused Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-test to derive the smallest useful tests for this changed behavior, then add them where the specs allow.

prompt_templates.title: Update Regression Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-test to identify the governing spec entries for this bug and add regression tests for the confirmed failure.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Spec Traceability
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-test to map tests to governing SpecDD entries, flag unverified requirements, and report any unclear write authority.

output_examples.input: Review tests for the checkout validation behavior.
output_examples.output: A coverage note linking existing tests to the relevant SpecDD entries, plus a short list of missing validation cases.

output_examples.input: Add tests for a bug fixed in account recovery.
output_examples.output: A focused regression test plan, edited test files when authority is clear, and a report of the spec entries covered.

output_examples.input: Check whether I can edit this test file under SpecDD rules.
output_examples.output: A concise authority decision that cites the nearest governing spec and explains whether edits are allowed.

best_practices: Start with the active bootstrap chain before resolving target specs.
Keep tests small and tied to observable behavior.
Report exact spec entries covered by each test.
anti_patterns: Using the skill in projects without a SpecDD bootstrap file.
Creating broad test harnesses when a focused test is enough.
Editing tests when no governing spec grants clear authority.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps AI coding agents derive and assess tests from active SpecDD project specifications.

faq.question: Does it work outside SpecDD projects?
faq.answer: No. It requires a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file before the workflow should continue.

faq.question: Can it create new tests?
faq.answer: Yes, when governing specs grant clear write authority for the relevant test files.

faq.question: Will it run tests?
faq.answer: It may run the relevant project test command when available and appropriate.

faq.question: How does it choose test cases?
faq.answer: It derives cases from SpecDD entries such as Must, Scenario, Handles, Returns, Raises, Tasks, and Done when.

faq.question: What should users review?
faq.answer: Users should review any generated tests, command execution, and claims about uncovered specified behavior.
